text:
Once upon an average dusk in a town known for its tranquility, there lived a peculiar old owl named Owley. Owley didn't sleep during the day. Quite the contrary; he spent it perched on a branch, watching the world go by with a wise old eye.

Every night, Owley's peculiar ritual began. He'd flap his wings gracefully and glide down from his ancient oak, heading straight to his secret garden. It wasn't a garden you'd expect. No blooms swayed gently; instead, it was a patch of damp dirt under a willow tree, littered with the shells of forgotten shells, the occasional bone, and, oddly, a small, old comic strip newspaper standing on its edge.

You see, Owley had a talent that most owls didn't possess: the ability to understand and speak basic human languages. He'd read the comics—archived and unread—to pass the time. They were his window to the outside world.

Dinnertime
